speaker: charlie
text: Now for some science that sounds like it's straight out of a sci-fi movie, but it's happening right now in labs around the world. Scientists at MIT have figured out how to turn chaotic laser light into a highly focused beam that can image the blood-brain barrier in 3D. This tech is 25 times faster than current methods.

speaker: alice
text: That's mind-blowing Alex, and speaking of breakthrough science, three researchers from Penn have just won the Breakthrough Prize for developing the first FDA-approved gene therapy for inherited blindness. We're talking about people who would've been completely blind by kindergarten now having their sight restored.

speaker: charlie
text: And get this - scientists have just completed the largest 3D map of the universe ever created using data from 47 million galaxies and quasars. This cosmic milestone could unlock new clues about dark energy and help us understand how the universe actually works. The scale of human curiosity and ingenuity just blows me away sometimes.
